title: Poems From "A Shropshire Lad" - XVIII
content:
Oh, when I was in love with you,
    Then I was clean and brave,
    And miles around the wonder grew
    How well did I behave.

    And now the fancy passes by,
    And nothing will remain,
    And miles around they'll say that I
    Am quite myself again.
meaning:
This evocative piece by Alfred Edward Housman, titled "Poems From "A Shropshire Lad" - XVIII", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
